demographics.living_situation: shares a flat with a flatmate

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My whole body is in agony — even my clothes hurt to wear — my brain feels completely blank, and I haven't slept in nearly two days because of the pain. My flatmate is with me and had to help me call. I don't know what to do, I can barely think straight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain has exploded over the last couple of days and the sleeplessness and brain fog are so bad I can't look after myself.

symptom_narrative.onset: it's been building up hard over the last 2 days
symptom_narrative.duration: the really bad flare is about 48 hours, but I live with this pain all the time
symptom_narrative.character: burning, aching pain all over — my skin is so tender that even a light touch or clothing hurts
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's ever been, honestly close to a 10 out of 10
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere — arms, legs, shoulders, back, all four sides of my body
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really helps right now, lying still is a bit less awful
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any touch, movement, or the weight of blankets or clothes makes it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my head feels foggy and blank, I can't remember what day it is or find my words, and I feel wrung out and tearful from no sleep
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get flares like this but usually milder — the fog and the pain have happened before, just never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed and my flatmate is helping me drink and talk to you — I'm scared I'll fall if I get up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing chronic widespread pain condition — fibromyalgia is what I've been told it is
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular paracetamol and trying to pace myself

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: the regular tablets, a couple when the pain is bad

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: not very active when I'm flaring — I try to move gently on better days
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live with a flatmate who is home and helping me right now

reluctance_topics: low mood and thoughts of self-harm

objective_data_offered.value: no fever — around 36.7 on the thermometer at home
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer, taken by the flatmate

objective_data_offered.value: about 95 beats per minute resting on my smartwatch
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single wearable reading while distressed and in pain

objective_data_offered.value: patient looks exhausted and tearful, speaks slowly, is moving very little; skin looks normal with no rash
flatmate says she is awake and talking but slow and hazy, knows who and where she is, and flinches from any touch
